title: Mid session 2901 T224 A
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2024

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test A

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 2

i.i.d. 1. [10 marks] Let (X1 , . . . , Xn ) ∼ Γ(α, λ), for some α, λ > 0.

a) [6 marks] Determine the exact distribution of the sample mean n 1X X̄n = Xi . n i=1

(Hint: use the properties of the moment generating function.)

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 3

b) [2 marks] Provide an approximation of this distribution based on the Central Limit Theorem and valid when n is ‘large’.

c) [2 marks] Verify that the exact distribution and the approximation share the same expectation and variance.

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 4

2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a bivariate discrete vector, with SX1 = {1, 2, 3}, SX2 = {1, 2, 3} and pX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = cx1 x2 for (x1 , x2 ) ∈ SX1 × SX2 and a constant c > 0.

a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 5

c) [3 marks] Find E(X1 ) and E(X2 ).

d) [2 marks] Find Cov(X1 , X2 ).

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 6

3. [10 marks] Suppose that the distribution of the weight of pre-packaged ‘1-kg’ bags of carrots is N (1.18, 0.072 ) and the distribution of the weight of prepackaged ‘3-kg’ bags of carrots is N (3.22, 0.092 ). Selecting bags at random, find the probability that the weight of three ‘1-kg’ bags exceeds the weight of one ‘3-kg’ bag. (Hint: Φ(−1.0322) = 0.1510, Φ(0.1510) = 0.5600, Φ(0.3200) = 0.6255, Φ(2.1193) = 0.9830.)
